title: What is the formal definition of software testing?

vocabulary.definition: The process of evaluating software to detect defects and verify that it meets specified requirements.
Checking whether we are building the product right according to specifications.
Checking whether we are building the right product for user needs.

transcript.en: Um, imagine writing a multi-million dollar check to build a bridge that you can't even see, right? Or touch or or even walk on. Yeah, exactly. You can't touch it until the exact moment, you know, a 100 heavy freight trains are just barreling across it. It's terrifying. It really is. Yeah. Because if there is a fatal structural flaw, you won't know until the steel snaps and the whole thing just collapses into the river below. And that I mean that terrifying scenario is basically the daily reality of modern software development,right? We build these massive invisible architectures out of code and logic and figuring out if they can actually hold the weight of real users is well, that's a monumental challenge. It's the ultimate invisible infrastructure really because when a physical bridge fails, you can see the rust, right? You can measure the stress points on the steel. But with software, with software, it might just be a a single misplaced comma buried in like 3 million lines of code. Yeah. short_answer.en: Software testing is the process of evaluating software to verify that it meets specified requirements and works as expected.
short_answer.ru: Тестирование программного обеспечения — это процесс оценки ПО, чтобы проверить, соответствует ли оно требованиям и работает ли ожидаемым образом.

practice_questions: How is testing different from debugging?
